title: sonchus arvensis var. maritimus
intro: Sonchus arvensis var. maritimus, also known as sea sowthistle, is a variety of the thistle plant that grows in coastal areas, characterized by its tall stem and yellow or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of sea sowthistle include its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of sea sowthistle include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Sea sowthistle can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting the active compounds with a solvent.
